data.title: NOVONIX sells battery tech unit to former CEO
data.description:
NOVONIX (NVX) on Thursday said it divested its Nova Scotia-based Battery Technology Solutions, or BTS, unit to the division's founder and the company's former CEO, Chris Burns.

The company said the transaction price was $1. The deal includes all assets and liabilities.

BTS will now operate as two independent companies: Avrion Battery Labs and Dryve Battery Materials.

NOVONIX will retain a 15% stake in a new cathode materials business to be called Dryve Battery Materials. The remaining operations will form Avrion Battery Labs, which will offer battery testing systems and research services.

The company said BTS will have about $2M in cash at closing, subject to adjustments. NOVONIX will also provide transition support and allow use of its trademark until the end-2026

BTS was founded by Burns in 2013 and acquired by NOVONIX in 2017.

“By divesting non-core business segments, we are directing our management attention and capital toward advancing domestic supply of this critical mineral and supporting the growth of the North American battery industry,” said CEO Mike O'Kronley.

NVX shares rose over +6% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Rein Therapeutics stock dips after pricing $50M share offering
data.description:
• Rein Therapeutics (RNTX) on Thursday said it priced an underwritten public offering of 50M shares at $1 each, raising $50M in gross proceeds.
  • The company granted underwriters a 45-day option to buy up to 7.5M additional shares to cover overallotments. The offering is expected to close around May 4.
  • The deal includes only common stock and no warrants.
  • Konik Capital Partners is acting as sole book-running manager.
  • Rein said it will use the proceeds to fund a Phase 2 trial of LTI-03 in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and for working capital. It expects the funds, along with existing cash, to support operations into 2028.
  • Shares fell -19.5% premarket on Friday.

data.title: U.S. seeks to deploy hypersonic missile for possible use against Iran - report
data.description:
The U.S. Central Command has requested to deploy the Dark Eagle long-range hypersonic missile to the Gulf region for possible use against Iran, Bloomberg News reported, citing a person with direct knowledge of the matter.

The request noted that Iran moved its launchers out of range of the Precision Strike Missile, which can hit targets at more than 300 miles, justifying the need for the hypersonic missile.

If approved, this would be the first time that the U.S. deployed its hypersonic missile, which hasn't yet been declared fully operational.

The request indicates that the U.S. is preparing for more strikes if the current ceasefire collapses with no deal to end the war. 

The missile component of the Dark Eagle is being developed by Lockheed Martin (LMT) and Northrop Grumman (NOC), according to the Congressional Research Service. Dynetics, a subsidiary of Leidos (LDOS), is under contract to build the hypersonic glide vehicle.

The Dark Eagle hypersonic missile has faced several delays over the years on account of testing failures and quality issues. To note, Russia and China have already deployed their own versions of the weapon.

data.title: Epsium Enterprise names new CFO
data.description:
• Epsium Enterprise (EPSM) said on Thursday it appointed Ching Wan Wong as CFO effective immediately, succeeding Ming Yin Gordon Au Yeung, who stepped down on April 30.
  • The company stated that Au Yeung’s departure was not due to any disagreement related to operations, policies, or practices.
  • The company said Wong brings more than 20 years of experience spanning financial reporting, internal controls, and regulatory compliance across U.S. and Hong Kong-listed firms.
  • Most recently, he served as CFO and Head of Reporting at Intelligent Living Application Group , where he oversaw SEC compliance and financial disclosures. His background also includes advisory work on Hong Kong Stock Exchange and U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission listing requirements.

data.title: Banner to acquire Pacific Financial in $177M all-stock deal
data.description:
Banner Corporation (BANR) and Pacific Financial Corporation (PFLC) on Friday said companies signed a merger agreement under which Banner will acquire Pacific Financial in an all-stock transaction, expanding its footprint across the Pacific Northwest.

Under the terms of the agreement, Pacific Financial shareholders will receive 0.2633 shares of Banner common stock for each share held. 

Based on Banner’s April 29 closing price of $66.25, the implied deal value stands at $17.44 per share, or approximately $177M in aggregate. Pacific shares closed at $13.48 on Thursday, implying a premium of around 30%.

The banks said the transaction was structured as a tax-free reorganization. Post-merger ownership is to be split with Pacific shareholders holding about 7% of the combined entity and Banner shareholders owning the remaining 93%.

“This transaction expands our presence and density in attractive Western Washington and Western Oregon markets," said Banner CEO Mark Grescovich.

The deal is projected to be immediately accretive to Banner’s 2027 earnings per share, excluding one-time costs.

Pacific Financial was advised by Piper Sandler and Miller Nash, while Banner was advised by BofA Securities and Ballard Spahr.

data.title: Amkor Technology prices $1B convertible debt offering
data.description:
Amkor Technology (AMKR) has priced its private offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031.

The sale is scheduled to settle on May 5, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. Amkor also granted the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $150M of notes.

The notes will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete. It will mature on July 15, 2031, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed, or converted.

The initial conversion rate is 9.4013 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, which represents an initial conversion price of around $106.37 per share of common stock - a premium of ~52.5% over the last reported sale price.

Amkor (AMKR) estimates net proceeds of around $981.7M, or ~$1,129M if the initial purchasers fully exercise their option to purchase additional notes. The firm plans to use $49M of the proceeds to fund the cost of entering into the capped call transactions and the remainder for general corporate purposes.

data.title: European markets subdued as most parts remain shut for Labor Day
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.52%. The pound climbed to $1.36 in early May, reaching its highest level since mid-February.

Germany (DAX:IND) closed for Labor Day.

France (CAC:IND) closed for Labor Day.

In other parts of Europe, retail sales in Switzerland increased by 0.5% Y/Y in March.

Retail sales in the Netherlands rose by 2.9% Y/Y in March 2026.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.11% lower to 610.6, dragged down by escalating concerns over the Middle East. WTI crude futures held above $105 per barrel on Friday and were set for a second weekly gain, amid dimming prospects for a US-Iran peace deal.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was almost flat at 4.39%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 2 basis points to 5.04%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 3.03%; markets remain shut.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Amgen raises 2026 guidance to $37.1B-$38.5B revenue and $21.70-$23.10 non-GAAP EPS while expanding MariTide Phase III switching studies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Amgen (AMGN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We had a strong first quarter and are well positioned to achieve our objectives for the year," said (Chairman & CEO Robert Bradway), reiterating that 2026 is "a springboard year" where the company expects "our rapidly growing products to offset the financial impact of patent expirations and increased competition."
  • Bradway highlighted that "our 6 key growth drivers...generated 70% of our sales in the quarter and grew in aggregate by 24%."
  • On MariTide, Bradway said Amgen is "disclosing additional Phase III studies...one of which will evaluate switching from the weekly injectables to MariTide on an every 8- or 12-week schedule" and will also evaluate "weight maintenance...on a schedule of 4 or 6 injections a year as well."
  • Bradway described an AI leadership transition: "we took steps early on to have Dave Reese lead these efforts" and "we're encouraged that Jay Bradner will build on Dave's accomplishments, leading our artificial intelligence and data activities across the company."
  • (Executive VP & CFO Peter Griffith) said, "We are pleased with our strong first quarter performance," reporting "non-GAAP operating margin was 45%" and "free cash flow" of "$1.5 billion."

Outlook

  • Griffith said, "we're raising our 2026 guidance ranges for both revenue and non-GAAP earnings per share," guiding to "total revenues in the range of $37.1 billion to $38.5 billion" and "non-GAAP earnings per share to be between $21.70 and $23.10," while noting, "our guidance does not include any potential business development transactions."
  • Compared with Q4 2025, Amgen’s ranges increased from "$37.0 billion to $38.4 billion" and "$21.60 to $23."
  • Griffith updated below-the-line assumptions to "non-GAAP OI&E...$2.2 billion to $2.3 billion of expense" and a "non-GAAP tax rate...15.0% to 16.5%."

Financial Results

  • Griffith reported "non-GAAP R&D spending increasing 16% year-over-year" and "non-GAAP cost of sales as a percentage of product sales was 19.5%," adding, "We expect these factors will continue to negatively impact the cost of sales in future quarters."
  • Griffith said, "We further expect the second quarter operating margin to be in line with the first quarter operating margin," and reiterated full-year expectations that "non-GAAP operating margin as a percentage of product sales" is "roughly 45% to 46%" and "share repurchases not to exceed $3 billion."
  • On capital allocation, Griffith said Amgen "spent $700 million...on capital expenditures" and still expects "approximately $2.6 billion" in 2026 capex, "including for MariTide's launch."
  • On taxes, Griffith warned that an IRS draft NOPA for 2016-2018 "could have a material impact on our financial statements" if sustained, while stating, "We firmly believe that the IRS positions are without merit."

Q&A

  • Yaron Werber, TD Cowen: Asked MariTide SWITCH design and non-inferiority; (Executive VP Bradner) said the study has "300 subjects," a "run-in on weekly semaglutide or tirzepatide," then switch to "every 8-week or a quarterly basis," with a primary endpoint of "change from baseline body weight after 52 weeks."
  • Salveen Richter, Goldman Sachs: Asked why no monthly arm and size of maintenance opportunity; Bradner said Amgen has "a lot of experience with monthly MariTide" and is focusing SWITCH on "every 8-week and every 12-week" learnings; (Executive VP Gordon) said the goal is to show how to "convert" from weekly agents and how to move maintenance to "Q8 week or...Q12 week."
  • RBC (for Luca Issi): Asked IMDELLTRA expansion and FDA real-time trial pilot; Bradner said Amgen is advancing into "frontline induction" and "maintenance" and working with FDA on a design leveraging "electronic health records and real-time data capture"; Gordon cited community uptake and "launching...across multiple markets this year."
  • Michael Yee, UBS: Asked olpasiran and background therapies; Bradner said, "you can't take a GLP-1...or even Repatha and meaningfully reduce levels of Lp(a)," describing OCEAN(a) as "almost 7,300 patients" with "95% reduction in Lp(a) with every 12-week dosing."
  • Terence Flynn, Morgan Stanley: Asked BD/M&A posture; Bradway said, "we're very active in business development" and will pursue opportunities where Amgen "can...add value to our shareholders."
  • Geoffrey Meacham, Citi: Asked Repatha primary prevention penetration and olpasiran market; Gordon said U.S. "new-to-brand prescription evolution...was up 44%" and Lp(a) testing guidance should help identify patients.
  • David Risinger, Leerink: Asked AmgenNow mix; Gordon said Repatha is offered at "$239 a month" and AmgenNow is "in the kind of the 8,000 to 9,000 patient range," but "as a percentage of total Repatha...it's relatively small."
  • Matthew Phipps, William Blair: Asked blinatumomab trial pauses; Bradner said Amgen "paused" enrollment after "a handful of inflammatory reactions" and is "having a dialogue with the FDA" and expects to reopen "shortly."
  • Christopher Schott, JPMorgan: Asked MariTide 3-step titration tolerability; Bradner said nausea/vomiting is "lower than we've seen before" and when present is "quite short in its duration."
  • Akash Tewari, Jefferies: Asked dazodalibep Phase III design and differentiation; Bradner said Sjogren’s heterogeneity drove two studies (ESSDAI and ESSPRI) and both are "expected to complete later this year."
  • Louise Chen, Scotiabank: Asked MariTide competitive ambition; Gordon said, "we're going to be the best monthly or less frequently prescribed agent," while Bradway said Amgen will "resist" ranking until it has "the data in hand."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were slightly positive and detail-oriented, pressing for trial design clarity (e.g., Werber asked for switching arms and "non-inferiority" specifics; Richter challenged why "not every 1 month").
  • Management tone was confident in growth drivers and pipeline, with repeated emphasis on differentiation (Bradway: "we're on track"; Bradner: "We remain confident and excited by MariTide"), while staying guarded on market-positioning questions (Bradway: "we'll resist and wait instead until we have the data in hand").
  • Versus Q4 2025, the tone added a more explicit risk disclosure on IRS matters (Griffith: "could have a material impact"), alongside stronger confidence via raised guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance moved higher: Q1 2026 raised 2026 revenue/EPS to "$37.1 billion to $38.5 billion" and "$21.70 to $23.10" from Q4 2025’s "$37.0 billion to $38.4 billion" and "$21.60 to $23."
  • R&D emphasis shifted from describing a broad 2026 data-generation year to detailing MariTide program expansion into switching/maintenance extensions and discontinuing a program (Bradner: "decision to discontinue development of AMG 193").
  • Risk framing expanded: Q1 introduced the IRS draft NOPA for 2016-2018 and said it "could have a material impact," a topic not described at the same level in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Griffith said an IRS draft NOPA for 2016-2018 asserts "significant adjustments" on profit allocation and "could have a material impact," while adding, "We disagree" and "intend to continue to vigorously defend our position."
  • Bradner said, "the FDA proposed to withdraw the approval of TAVNEOS," adding, "We are confident in the benefit risk profile...and expect to engage further with the FDA."
  • Griffith cautioned that cost-of-sales pressures from "higher profit share and royalty expenses and changes in our sales mix" are expected to "continue to negatively impact the cost of sales in future quarters."

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 2026 as a strong start to a "springboard year," driven by "6 key growth drivers" that grew "24%" in aggregate and supported a raise to full-year 2026 revenue and non-GAAP EPS guidance. Executives emphasized MariTide’s push toward less frequent dosing, including new Phase III switching and maintenance extensions, alongside continued investment (capex and higher R&D) and active business development interest. The call also elevated two notable uncertainties: ongoing TAVNEOS regulatory pressure and a newly disclosed IRS draft adjustment that management said could be material if sustained, while stating Amgen will vigorously contest it.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stock index futures muted as investors look to start May on optimistic note
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted as Wall Street looked to kick off the month of May on an optimistic note.

Dow futures (INDU) rose 0.13%, while S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged up 0.15% and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) slipped 0.14%.

Apple (AAPL) gained 3% in premarket trade after the technology giant reported quarterly results that were above Wall Street’s forecast. Investors were looking ahead to PMI Manufacturing Final and ISM Manufacturing Index data.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2.2 basis points to 4.40%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 2.5 basis points to 3.90%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) advanced 2 basis points to 4.99%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included TKO Group (TKO) +3.99%, GoDaddy (GDDY) +3.01%, and Labcorp (LH) +3.00%. Decliners included Western Digital (WDC) -8.17%, Clorox (CLX) -7.23%, and Sandisk (SNDK) -5.97%.

data.title: Reddit outlines $715M-$725M Q2 2026 revenue while targeting 100M daily U.S. users
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Reddit (RDDT) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “We’re excited to start the year off with a strong first quarter,” said (Co-Founder, CEO, President & Director Steven Huffman), highlighting “our seventh consecutive quarter with revenue growth over 60%,” “industry-leading gross margins over 90%,” “adjusted EBITDA margin of 40%,” and “record cash flow of more than $300 million,” alongside “capital expenditures remained low as $1 million.”

  • (CEO Huffman) tied the ads story to intent on-platform, saying “around 40% of conversations on Reddit are commercial in nature,” and positioned data licensing/AI demand as a scarcity asset: “Reddit’s conversations are like oil for the modern Internet.”

  • (CEO Huffman) set a specific U.S. DAU goal and framed it as a product execution agenda: “Our goal is to reach 100 million daily U.S. users,” adding, “one thing that has become clear is that product quality leads to growth,” with near-term focus including “broadening the top of funnel, improving new user retention and making Reddit faster across the board.”

  • (Chief Operating Officer Jennifer Wong) emphasized performance advertising and automation, reporting “total revenue grew 69% year-over-year to $663 million,” “advertising revenue grew 74% year-over-year to $625 million,” and “performance-oriented revenue represents over 60% of total ad revenue.”

  • (COO Wong) described early Reddit Max results: “On average, advertisers are seeing a 17% reduction in cost per action and 25% more conversion outcomes when running Max campaigns,” and said “about 50% of Max campaign advertisers” used “AI-powered creative features.”

  • (Chief Financial Officer Andrew Vollero) anchored the quarter on profitability and cash generation: “Reddit’s Q1 47% free cash flow margin was a powerful proof point,” and “on a GAAP basis, EPS reached triple digits in Q1 at $1.01 a share.”

Outlook

  • (CFO Vollero) guided Q2 2026 revenue to “$715 million to $725 million,” and said the range “takes into account the lapping of a particularly strong growth period in Q2 2025.”

  • (CFO Vollero) guided Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA to “$285 million to $295 million,” and said “the Q2 guide assumes a total adjusted cost basis of $430 million.”

  • (CFO Vollero) flagged expected SBC seasonality: “We anticipate our Q2 stock-based compensation-related tax expense to be sequentially higher than Q1,” while also stating “year-over-year stock-based comp expenses could grow about half the rate of revenue for the quarter.”

Financial Results

  • (CFO Vollero) reported Q1 revenue of “$663 million,” with “ad revenue…$625 million,” and “other revenue…reached $39 million,” while adding “ARPU grew 44% year-over-year to $5.23.”

  • (CFO Vollero) reported profitability and margins, including “gross margins were 91.5%,” “net income was $204 million,” and “$1.07 per basic share and $1.01 per diluted share.”

  • (CFO Vollero) detailed cost structure and hiring pace: “total adjusted costs…were $397 million,” “cost of revenue…was $56 million,” “operating expenses…were $341 million,” and “we added about 32 net people in Q1.”

  • (CFO Vollero) highlighted capital allocation capacity: “We ended Q1 with $2.8 billion in cash and investments,” and said buybacks were limited in the quarter as “share repurchase activity was modest…about 35,000 shares,” with “about $995 million remains on our $1 billion authorization.”

Q&A

  • Douglas Anmuth, JPMorgan: Asked what will most impact user frequency and DPA adoption; (CEO Huffman) said “the biggest drivers will probably be performance,” and added “Search WAUqs are up 30% year-over-year,” while (COO Wong) said “there’s still thousands of advertisers that can adopt DPA that haven’t adopted yet.”

  • Josh Beck, Raymond James: Asked for ML priorities and top-of-funnel strategy; (CEO Huffman) said feed ML is “pretty much the entire stack” and “my answer is everything,” while (COO Wong) said search is a key top-of-funnel and marketing is used to “prime the market to increase that awareness.”

  • Ronald Josey, Citi: Asked about bot labeling/verification and Reddit Max learnings; (CEO Huffman) said “these things are actually all overlapping,” citing Passkeys and “bot verification,” while (COO Wong) said customers like that Max “doesn’t feel black boxy to them.”

  • Jason Helfstein, Oppenheimer: Pressed on DAU priority and AI-driven DAU attribution; (CEO Huffman) responded “DAU is the primary focus of the company,” and on partners added “nothing new to share.”

  • Richard Greenfield, LightShed: Pressed for a timeline on “100 million” U.S. DAU and AI deal economics; (CEO Huffman) said “I don’t know the time line,” and on exclusivity added “No comment on that.”

  • Mark Shmulik, Bernstein: Asked when talent/process changes show in KPIs and whether ad load risks engagement; (CEO Huffman) said “we are really in the middle of it” and “we do expect to see improvements…immediately,” while (COO Wong) said “our ad load overall is still quite low compared to peers.”

  • Thomas Champion, Piper Sandler: Asked logged-in vs. logged-out monetization and ad-market volatility; (COO Wong) said impression value is “pretty equal,” and on macro said “we haven’t seen anything acute,” but noted “shorter cycles…month-to-month.”

  • Mark Mahaney, Evercore: Asked about engagement measures and Reddit Max adoption; (CEO Huffman) cited “new user retention” and frequency histograms where “the 2 tallest bars will be 1 day and 7 days,” while (COO Wong) said “it’s still early” and “it was launched in January.”

  • John Colantuoni, Jefferies: Asked international translation effects and logged-in personalization; (CEO Huffman) said “every market is different,” and that long-term “what’s most important is getting more native communities,” while adding logged-in personalization “improves retention and time spent.”

  • Justin Post, BofA: Asked genAI use of Reddit data and Google algorithm changes; (CEO Huffman) said “Reddit has been for a while and continues to be the most cited source in AI citations,” and on search traffic shifts said “nothing further to comment on.”

  • Benjamin Black, Deutsche Bank: Asked contribution health and partner-selection criteria for licensing; (CEO Huffman) pointed to “post success rate” and “post guidance,” and said partnerships extend beyond dollars to “bringing in new users” and “advancing our own AI technology.”

  • Naved Khan, B. Riley: Asked AnswerPlus impact and international monetization coverage; (CEO Huffman) said “search…all up meaningfully year-over-year,” and (COO Wong) outlined direct sales coverage in “the U.S., Canada, U.K., covering Continental Europe…as well as Australia.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly negative to neutral, with pointed challenges on AI deal value and DAU mix (e.g., Richard Greenfield, LightShed: “$50 million to $60 million a year…seems like a pimple”).

  • Management tone in prepared remarks was positive and confident (CEO Huffman: “strong first quarter”), while Q&A mixed confidence with constraint on sensitive items (CEO Huffman: “I don’t know the time line,” and “No comment on that”).

  • Versus the prior quarter’s emphasis on “a $1 billion share repurchase program,” the current call leaned more into scaling DAU and product execution, while maintaining margin/cash flow confidence (CFO Vollero: “results stand alone in a very positive way”).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 centered on “Our goal is to reach 100 million daily U.S. users” and product levers (feed ML, onboarding, performance), while Q4 introduced leadership and capital return, including “Maria Angelidou-Smith as our new Chief Product Officer” and the “$1 billion share repurchase program.”

  • Q1 expanded operational details on Reddit Max and shopping partnerships, including “an integration with Shopify,” while Q4 framed Max as a “public beta launch” and positioned 2026 as the year to “scale automation.”

  • Q1 reiterated the planned reporting change: “2026 will be the last period we disclosed logged in and logged out DAUq metrics,” consistent with Q4’s disclosure phaseout plan beginning “in Q3 2026.”

Risks and Concerns

  • (COO Wong) cited demand-planning uncertainty: “some are planning on shorter cycles…month-to-month,” while adding “no material change in their commitments.”

  • (CEO Huffman) framed bot/spam defenses as ongoing, saying “this is…part of the human verification and defense of Reddit,” and described efforts spanning “bot verification,” “Passkeys,” and “unauthorized…bot usage.”

  • (CFO Vollero) pointed to execution sensitivity around retention and marketing efficiency: “user retention remains an opportunity and an important unlocker to improving investment returns and marketing.”

Final Takeaway

Reddit’s management framed Q1 as a combination of rapid revenue scaling and high margins alongside a sharpened product agenda, repeatedly tying long-term upside to increasing U.S. daily usage (“Our goal is to reach 100 million daily U.S. users”) through improvements in feed relevance, onboarding, and app performance. The company guided Q2 to $715 million to $725 million in revenue and $285 million to $295 million of adjusted EBITDA, while reiterating that advertiser automation (Reddit Max), shopping formats, and partner integrations are central to expanding performance outcomes amid a more month-to-month ad-planning backdrop.
